summary: The weak pentagon conjecture — that every cubic triangle-free graph admits a homomorphism to the Clebsch graph — remains open in full generality. The key partial result at posting time (girth $\geq 17$ suffices, DeVos–Šámal) was already cited in the problem. Subsequent searches strongly suggest that further partial progress has been made (triangle-free planar graphs, $K_5$-minor-free graphs), but every candidate post-posting paper was inaccessible via WebFetch (HTTP 403 / paywall), so none can be cited per verification requirements.
search_queries: weak pentagon problem cubic triangle-free graph Clebsch homomorphism conjecture Samal
cubic graph homomorphism Clebsch graph triangle-free cut-continuous mapping C5 pentagon

notes: Multiple independent search results consistently assert that Naserasr proved every triangle-free planar graph maps homomorphically to the Clebsch graph (J. Graph Theory 73, 2013, DOI 10.1002/jgt.21703), and that a 2009 Discrete Mathematics paper (pii S0012365X09002581) extended this to triangle-free K5-minor-free graphs — both post-posting partial results. However, the Wiley journal page, IRIF author PDF, ResearchGate, and HAL all returned HTTP 403 or anti-bot blocks, making it impossible to confirm titles/authors/claims per the verification requirement. The arXiv preprint math/0602580 (DeVos–Šámal, Feb 2006) predates the posting and is already cited in the OPG statement as [DS]. The signed projective cubes paper arXiv:2406.10814 (Chen–Naserasr–Sarti, 2024) was verified but does not directly address the weak pentagon conjecture for cubic triangle-free graphs.
